title: Electric Vehicle Charging
description: Additive module for EV charging station-related physical security, operations, and governance considerations.

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Implement RFID Access Control Addresses: May lead to tampering with charging infrastructure, resulting in data breaches or service disruptions.

sources.label: Cyber Threats to EV Charging Infrastructure - SEC Consult (January 15, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Install Surveillance Cameras Addresses: May lead to tampering with charging infrastructure, resulting in data breaches or service disruptions.

sources.label: Electric Vehicle Charger Security Risks - VicOne (August 26, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Conduct Regular Security Audits Addresses: May lead to tampering with charging infrastructure, resulting in data breaches or service disruptions.

sources.label: Sandia Studies Vulnerabilities of Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure (November 15, 2022)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Enhance Lighting Around Stations Addresses: Increased likelihood of vandalism or theft due to lack of visibility, making installations attractive targets for criminal activity.

sources.label: Improving Public EV Charger Locations - GreenTech (March 12, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Design Stations with Visibility in Mind Addresses: Increased likelihood of vandalism or theft due to lack of visibility, making installations attractive targets for criminal activity.

sources.label: Public Space Design - Urban Planning Journal (July 18, 2023)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Install Panic Buttons or Alert Systems Addresses: Increased likelihood of vandalism or theft due to lack of visibility, making installations attractive targets for criminal activity.

sources.label: Modern Security Solutions for EV Chargers - TechSecure (June 21, 2024)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Use Tamper-Proof Hardware Addresses: Physical tampering can lead to equipment damage and hazardous situations, compromising user safety.

sources.label: How to Address the Increasing Threat of Cyberattacks on EV Charging Stations - Virta (May 14, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Regular Maintenance Checks Addresses: Physical tampering can lead to equipment damage and hazardous situations, compromising user safety.

sources.label: Charging Station Security Standards - Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Alliance (March 10, 2023)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Create Awareness Training for Staff Addresses: Physical tampering can lead to equipment damage and hazardous situations, compromising user safety.

sources.label: The Importance of Staff Training in EV Charger Security - ChargeLab (October 02, 2024)

risk_drivers.driver_text: Data Theft and Skimming: Install Encryption for Transactions — Potential for identity theft and fraud, leading to financial losses for users and damage to trust in the charging infrastructure.
risk_drivers.reference: Cybersecurity Best Practices for EV Charging Stations - Connect EV (February 17, 2025)

risk_drivers.driver_text: Data Theft and Skimming: Utilize Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) — Potential for identity theft and fraud, leading to financial losses for users and damage to trust in the charging infrastructure.
risk_drivers.reference: Securing Payment Systems for EV Chargers - FinTech Research Group (August 25, 2023)

risk_drivers.driver_text: Data Theft and Skimming: Monitor Network Traffic for Anomalies — Potential for identity theft and fraud, leading to financial losses for users and damage to trust in the charging infrastructure.
risk_drivers.reference: Cybersecurity Risks in EV Charging - Driivz (May 19, 2025)
